summary: A deep, full‑bodied audience recording captures a relaxed 1976 show with standout performances of Mission In The Rain and Lazy Lightning > Supplication.
review: Both reviewers praise this June ’76 tape for its unusually rich audience sound – deeper and fuller than other crowd recordings – and for the laid‑back, under‑appreciated vibe of the band that year. The setlist is highlighted with Mission In The Rain, Lazy Lightning > Supplication, Might As Well and a soaring Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence. Drummer Mickey Hart’s presence is noted as fresh energy as the group settles into its new chemistry.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently positive 4‑star reviews emphasize both a high‑quality audience recording and an enjoyable, relaxed performance; limited to two detailed reviews, giving moderate confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as the deepest, fullest sounding AUD tape of the three available; clear enough for a crowd source with good balance and minimal noise complaints.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Setlist praised for its relaxed feel and smooth transitions, showcasing a band comfortable in its laid‑back groove.

band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart is noted as a new member still feeling each other out, adding fresh energy to the performance.
